researched_category.notes: Meta Ads is a digital advertising platform for creating, managing, and optimizing ads across Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and WhatsApp.

one_liner.value: Meta's advertising platform for creating, managing, and optimizing ad campaigns across Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and WhatsApp via the Marketing API (Graph API).

methods.notes: Primary auth is OAuth2 (Meta Business OAuth for the new MCP/CLI connectors; standard OAuth2 for the Marketing API). Also supports access tokens (short-lived user tokens, long-lived user tokens, and system user tokens) which function like API keys.

access.notes: The Marketing API itself is free (no per-call charges). Developers can create a free Meta Developer account and get Limited (Development) Access immediately. However, Full Access (production) requires App Review, meeting criteria of 500+ API calls in 15 days and <15% error rate. The new Meta Ads AI Connectors (MCP/CLI, launched April 29, 2026) bypass the traditional Developer App flow using Meta Business OAuth and are free during open beta. Business Verification may also be required for some use cases.

summary.value: The Meta Marketing API is a well-documented, extensive REST API built on the Graph API. It covers campaign management, ad creation, audience targeting, insights/reporting, creative management, catalog management, business management, and more. The API has official SDKs for Python, PHP, JavaScript, and other languages. Current version is v25.0 (as of June 2026). Also supports Webhooks for real-time event streaming.

agent_interface.notes: Meta launched an official MCP server on April 29, 2026 (mcp.facebook.com/ads) with 29 tools, free in open beta, authenticated via Meta Business OAuth. Also launched an official CLI. Community MCP servers exist (pipeboard-co/meta-ads-mcp with 187 stars, hashicott/meta-ads-mcp-server). Composio has a Meta Ads toolkit with 52 tools supporting OAUTH2 and API_KEY auth.

rationale.value: Meta Ads is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has a well-documented public REST API (Marketing API via Graph API), supports OAuth2 authentication, and offers multiple integration paths: direct API calls with access tokens, an official MCP server (mcp.facebook.com/ads, 29 tools, free beta), and an official CLI. Composio already provides a 52-tool Meta Ads toolkit. The API is free to use; however, production-level rate limits require passing App Review (500+ API calls in 15 days, <15% error rate) to achieve Full Access tier. The new MCP/CLI connectors launched April 29, 2026, simplify setup by using Meta Business OAuth and bypassing the traditional Developer App flow. No material blocker exists for building an agent toolkit today.

s1.quote: The Marketing API is a collection of Graph API endpoints and other features that can be used to help you advertise across Meta technologies.
s1.title: Marketing API | Meta Developer Documentation

s2.quote: Business apps are subject to an additional layer of Graph API authorization called access levels. During App Review, your app must also request specific permissions and features.
s2.title: Authorization - Meta Marketing API

s3.quote: The Graph API is the primary way for apps to read and write to the Facebook social graph. All of our SDKs and products interact with the Graph API in some way.
s3.title: Graph API Documentation

s4.quote: Limited access (default): Heavily rate-limited per ad account. For development only. Not for production apps running for live advertisers. Full access (after App Review): Lightly rate limited per ad account.
s4.title: Authorization - Marketing API Access Tier overview

s5.quote: In order to get Full access of Marketing API Access Tier, your app needs to meet these requirements: Have successfully made at least 500 Marketing API calls in the last 15 days. Have made Marketing API calls with an error rate of less than 15% in the last 500 calls.
s5.title: Authorization - Get Full access

s6.quote: Meta uses an OAuth permission system. Your app needs specific scopes approved to use the Ads API. The two essential permissions are: ads_read, ads_management.
s6.title: Authorization - Marketing API

s7.quote: For the Marketing API, there are no per-call costs but there is a rate-limit budget. Each call costs points, with reads at 1 point and writes at 3 points.
s7.title: Facebook API Pricing: Full Breakdown for 2026

s8.quote: This is a full list of root nodes for the Facebook Marketing API with links to reference docs for each.
s8.title: Marketing API Reference

s10.quote: Meta Ads AI Connectors is Meta's umbrella name for two interfaces to the Marketing API: an MCP Server (hosted at mcp.facebook.com/ads for conversational use) and a CLI (installed via npm for scripted workflows). Both launched April 29, 2026.
s10.title: Meta's MCP and CLI: What Advertisers Need to Know in 2026

s11.quote: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that lets AI assistants run your Meta Ads end to end — launch campaigns, upload creatives, update budgets, and dig into performance through natural conversation.
s11.title: pipeboard-co/meta-ads-mcp

s12.quote: A Model Context Protocol server for the Meta (Facebook) Ads API, written in TypeScript. 54 tools — 35 read tools (always on) plus 19 opt-in write/lifecycle tools.
s12.title: Meta Ads MCP Server

s13.quote: Connect your AI agents and chatbots (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc) with Metaads MCP or direct API to create campaigns, pull ad performance data, update budgets, and generate custom reports through natural language.
s13.title: Metaads MCP Integration for AI Agents - Composio

s14.quote: MCP - Connect AI agents to Meta MCP servers
s14.title: Meta Developer Documentation

limitations: The official Meta blog announcement post for the MCP/CLI connectors (developers.facebook.com/blog/post/2026/04/29/meta-ads-ai-connectors-mcp-cli/) returned empty content, but multiple independent secondary sources confirmed the launch and details.
